channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Iran Ceasefire Deal on Massive Life Support, Trump Rejects Counteroffer
item.description: President Trump criticized Iran ceasefire proposal as unbelievably weak as the month-long truce hangs by a thread. The US is pushing for a more comprehensive agreement amid concerns about escalation.

item.title: Hamas Weaponized Sexual Violence in October 7 Attacks, Israeli Investigation Finds
item.description: A comprehensive Israeli report documents systematic sexual assaults during the October 7 attacks, detailing harrowing cases. It is the most extensive investigation of its kind to date.

item.title: Russian Ship That Sank Near Spain May Have Been Carrying Nuclear Reactors to North Korea
item.description: The cargo vessel Ursa Major, which sank off the Spanish coast, may have been transporting nuclear reactor components destined for North Korea, raising questions about Russia-North Korea military cooperation.

item.title: UAEs Secret Attack on Iran Risks Drawing Gulf States Into Regional War
item.description: The UAE carried out a covert strike on Iran during the ongoing conflict, raising concerns that Gulf states could become targets if the fragile ceasefire collapses.

item.title: Global Conflict Displacements Hit Record 32.3 Million in 2025
item.description: For the first time, displacements from violence and conflict exceeded those from disasters, with 82.2 million people displaced worldwide. The surge is driven by conflicts in Ukraine, Sudan, and the Middle East.

item.title: Zelenskys Former Second-in-Command Yermak Charged in Major Corruption Probe
item.description: Ukraine's anti-corruption agencies have named Andriy Yermak, former chief of staff to President Zelensky, as a suspect in a money-laundering scheme, marking a significant escalation in corruption investigations.

item.title: Sweden Detains Two Suspects for Aiding High-Tech Shipments to Russia
item.description: Swedish authorities have detained two individuals suspected of facilitating the shipment of high-tech goods to Russia, potentially violating sanctions.

item.title: UK Resolutely Behind Ukraine, Minister Tells Kyiv Independent
item.description: A UK minister reaffirmed Britain's unwavering support for Ukraine during an exclusive interview with the Kyiv Independent.

item.title: Trump-Class Battleships Will Be Nuclear Powered
item.description: The US Navy's new Trump-class battleships will feature nuclear propulsion, marking a significant shift in surface fleet capabilities.

item.title: Israel Defense Developments Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Israel continues to advance defense capabilities amid ongoing regional tensions and security challenges.

item.title: Trump and Xi Aim to Keep Iran War Differences From Overshadowing China Summit
item.description: President Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping appear intent on preventing deep disagreements over the Iran war from derailing their upcoming summit meeting.

item.title: US Inflation Jumps to 3.8 Percent as Iran War Drives Energy Costs Higher
item.description: US consumer prices rose 3.8% in April, the highest level since May 2023, as the Iran war continues to push energy costs upward.

item.title: Xi Poised to Press Trump on Taiwan Arms Sales at Summit
item.description: Beijing has called Taiwan the core of China's core interests. Xi Jinping is likely to focus on getting President Trump to slow approval of more weapons for the self-governing island.

item.title: Israel Killing at Least One Child a Week in Occupied West Bank, UN Says
item.description: A UN report finds that Israeli forces have killed at least one Palestinian child per week in the occupied West Bank, raising concerns about escalating violence.

item.title: Dali Ship Operator Charged Over Deadly Baltimore Bridge Collapse
item.description: Six construction workers were killed and several vehicles plunged into the river after the cargo ship Dali crashed into the Francis Scott Key Bridge in Baltimore.

item.title: NATO to Cultivate Vetted Counter-Drone Vendor Pool
item.description: NATO is developing a pool of pre-vetted counter-drone vendors that member nations can select from, streamlining procurement of drone defense systems.

item.title: Turkeys Aselsan Eyes Growth in Networked Mass-Producible Weapons
item.description: Turkish defense company Aselsan is focusing on expanding its portfolio of networked, mass-producible weapons systems, according to its CEO.

item.title: US Navy Open to Building Ships Overseas, New Plan Says
item.description: A new Navy plan indicates openness to building ships at overseas shipyards, a significant shift in US naval procurement strategy.

item.title: Major Japanese Petroleum Company Expects Hormuz Crisis to Subside in July
item.description: A major Japanese oil company anticipates that the Strait of Hormuz disruption will ease by July, providing some relief to energy markets.

item.title: Russia Keeps Attacking US Firms in Ukraine, White House Silent
item.description: Facilities tied to Coca-Cola, Cargill, Mondelez and others appear to have been deliberately hit by Russian forces. The Trump administration's muted response has raised concerns.

item.title: Supreme Court Halts Order for Alabama to Use US House Map With 2 Black Districts
item.description: The Supreme Court has blocked a lower court ruling that would have required Alabama to draw a congressional map with two majority-Black districts.

item.title: Venezuelas Acting President Rejects Trumps 51st State Remarks
item.description: Venezuela's acting president defended the country's territory and rejected comments by Trump suggesting Venezuela could become the 51st US state.

item.title: Ugandas Museveni Sworn In for Record Seventh Term as President
item.description: Yoweri Museveni, 81, won disputed elections in January to extend his 40-year rule, cementing his position as one of Africa's longest-serving leaders.

item.title: WHO Head Warns Countries to Prepare for More Hantavirus Cases
item.description: The World Health Organization director-general urged countries to prepare for additional hantavirus cases as the outbreak spreads from a cruise ship.

item.title: UK Starmer Defies Calls to Resign After Heavy Election Losses
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer vowed to continue in office as he met with cabinet members. Dozens of Labour Party lawmakers had urged him to step down after heavy losses in local elections.
